sets.q: A runner displaces 200 m north in 25 seconds. What is their average velocity?
sets.given:
Formula: v = Δx / t
Velocity is a vector — include direction.

sets.q: A bicycle travels 150 m east in 30 seconds. What is its average velocity?
A boat moves 240 m south in 60 seconds. What is its average velocity?
A car travels 360 m west in 40 seconds. What is its average velocity?
A swimmer covers 100 m north in 50 seconds. What is the average velocity?
What is the displacement of an object that moves at 5 m/s for 12 seconds?
An object moves at 8 m/s for 10 seconds. What is its displacement?
A car travels at 12 m/s for 15 seconds. How far does it go?
A train moves at 20 m/s for 45 seconds. What is the displacement?
A person jogs at 3 m/s for 8 minutes. How far do they travel?
sets.given: Δx = v × t (convert time to seconds: 8 min = 480 s)

sets.q: A train goes from 20 m/s to 80 m/s in 15 seconds. What is its acceleration?
A motorcycle accelerates from 10 m/s to 50 m/s in 8 seconds. What is its acceleration?
A sprinter goes from rest to 9 m/s in 3 seconds. What is the acceleration?
A bus accelerates from 5 m/s to 25 m/s in 10 seconds. What is the acceleration?
A rocket increases speed from 0 to 120 m/s in 12 seconds. What is the acceleration?
A car decelerates from 30 m/s to 0 m/s in 6 seconds. What is its acceleration?
sets.given:
Deceleration = negative acceleration.
Formula: a = (vf − vi) / t

sets.q: A skateboard slows from 12 m/s to 0 m/s in 4 seconds. What is the acceleration?
sets.given: a = (vf − vi) / t. Slowing down means negative acceleration.

sets.q: A ball rolling on the floor slows from 8 m/s to 2 m/s in 3 seconds. What is the acceleration?
A truck brakes from 20 m/s to 5 m/s in 5 seconds. What is its acceleration?
A cyclist decelerates from 15 m/s to 3 m/s in 6 seconds. What is the acceleration?
An object starts from rest and accelerates at 6 m/s² for 3 seconds. What is its final velocity?
sets.given:
Formula: vf = vi + a·t
Starts from rest means vi = 0.

sets.q: A stone is dropped and falls freely. After 4 seconds, what is its velocity? (g = 10 m/s²)
sets.given: vf = vi + a·t, vi = 0, a = 10 m/s²

sets.q: A car starts from rest and accelerates at 4 m/s² for 5 seconds. What is its final velocity?
A rocket starts from rest and accelerates at 15 m/s² for 6 seconds. What is the final speed?
A ball is released from rest and rolls down a ramp with acceleration 3 m/s² for 7 seconds. What is the final velocity?
An object moving at 10 m/s accelerates at 3 m/s² for 4 seconds. What is its final velocity?
A boat moving at 6 m/s accelerates at 2 m/s² for 5 seconds. What is the final velocity?
A car travelling at 15 m/s accelerates at 4 m/s² for 3 seconds. What is the new speed?
A train moving at 25 m/s accelerates at 1.5 m/s² for 8 seconds. What is the final velocity?
A plane at 80 m/s accelerates at 5 m/s² for 10 seconds before takeoff. What is the final speed?
A ball starts from rest and rolls with an acceleration of 2 m/s² for 5 seconds. What distance does it cover?
sets.given:
Formula: Δx = vi·t + ½·a·t²
Starts from rest means vi = 0.

sets.q: A car starts from rest and accelerates at 3 m/s² for 4 seconds. How far does it travel?
A stone is dropped from rest and falls for 3 seconds. How far does it fall? (g = 10 m/s²)
An object starts from rest and accelerates at 5 m/s² for 6 seconds. What is the displacement?
A sled starts from rest and accelerates at 1.5 m/s² for 8 seconds. What distance does it cover?
What is the difference between displacement and distance?
sets.given:
Distance is a scalar — total path length.
Displacement is a vector — straight-line change in position with direction.
opts: Displacement is a vector with direction; distance is a scalar with no direction
Distance is a vector; displacement is a scalar
Displacement is always larger than distance

sets.q: A student walks 4 m east then 3 m west. What is their distance and displacement?
sets.given: Distance = total path length. Displacement = net change in position.

sets.q: Which of the following is a scalar quantity in kinematics?
sets.given: Scalars have magnitude only. Vectors have magnitude and direction.

sets.q: A runner completes a 400 m circular track and returns to the starting line. What is the displacement?
sets.given: Displacement = straight-line distance from start to finish.

sets.q: Which best describes displacement?
sets.given: Displacement is a vector quantity in kinematics.
opts: The straight-line distance from start to finish, with direction
The rate of change of velocity

sets.q: A car travels at constant velocity of 20 m/s for 8 seconds. What is its displacement?
sets.given:
At constant velocity, acceleration = 0.
Formula: Δx = v × t

sets.q: A train moves at constant 30 m/s for 12 seconds. What is its displacement?
A drone flies at constant velocity 15 m/s for 10 seconds. What distance does it cover?
A ship steams at a constant 8 m/s for 45 seconds. What is the displacement?
A conveyor belt moves items at a steady 2 m/s. How far does an item travel in 3 minutes?
An object has vi = 4 m/s, a = 2 m/s², and t = 3 s. What is its displacement?
An object has vi = 6 m/s, a = 3 m/s², and t = 4 s. What is the displacement?
An object has vi = 2 m/s, a = 4 m/s², and t = 5 s. What is the displacement?
An object has vi = 10 m/s, a = 1 m/s², and t = 6 s. What is its displacement?
An object has vi = 0 m/s, a = 5 m/s², and t = 4 s. What is the displacement?
